{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Shimoyama Kofun | native_name =下山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki).jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shimoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|07|57.6|N|131|47|33|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Usuki, Ōita]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|07|57.6|N|131|47|33|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Shimoyama Kofun'''|下山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Shibao neighborhood of the city of [[Usuki, Ōita]], on the island of [[Kyushu]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/203066|title= 下山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shimoyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a hill at an elevation of approximately 70 meters overlooking the Kumazaki River. Its total length is 68 meters, with a posterior circular portion 46-meters in diameter, and a rectangular anterior portion 37 meters wide. Traces of a partial moat encircle the posterior portion and there is a ''baizuka'' ancillary tumulus. Near the boundary between the anterior and posterior portions, is a heavily damaged stone statue depicting warrior in armor. There are only two ''kofun'' in Ōita Prefecture that have such a statue: Shimoyama Kofun, and Usuzuka Kofun, also located in Usuki. In the posterior circular portion is a house-shaped sarcophagus measuring 2.5 meters long, 1.3 meters wide, and 1 meter high, the lid of which has a band pattern embossed on it. When [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1951, the remains of two bodies, one male and one female, were found within the coffin, and the [[grave goods]] included a divine beast [[bronze mirror]], a shell ring, cylindrical beads, a comb, an iron sword and iron arrowheads, an iron ax, etc. A large amount of iron material, believed to be unprocessed iron, was found outside the coffin. From these grave goods it is estimated that it was built in the middle of the 5th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Currently, a shelter has been placed over the sarcophagus to permit public viewing. The tumulus is approximately a 15-minute walk from [[Kumasaki Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Nippō Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.usuki.oita.jp/docs/2014020500936/ Usuki City home page]{{in lang|ja}}

{{nihongo|'''Shimoyama Kofun'''|下山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Shibao neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Usuki, Ōita|Usuki, Ōita]], on the island of [[Kyushu]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/203066|title= 下山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shimoyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a hill at an elevation of approximately 70 meters overlooking the Kumazaki River. Its total length is 68 meters, with a posterior circular portion 46-meters in diameter, and a rectangular anterior portion 37 meters wide. Traces of a partial moat encircle the posterior portion and there is a ''baizuka'' ancillary tumulus. Near the boundary between the anterior and posterior portions, is a heavily damaged stone statue depicting warrior in armor. There are only two ''kofun'' in Ōita Prefecture that have such a statue: Shimoyama Kofun, and Usuzuka Kofun, also located in Usuki. In the posterior circular portion is a house-shaped sarcophagus measuring 2.5 meters long, 1.3 meters wide, and 1 meter high, the lid of which has a band pattern embossed on it. When [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1951, the remains of two bodies, one male and one female, were found within the coffin, and the [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included a divine beast [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]], a shell ring, cylindrical beads, a comb, an iron sword and iron arrowheads, an iron ax, etc. A large amount of iron material, believed to be unprocessed iron, was found outside the coffin. From these grave goods it is estimated that it was built in the middle of the 5th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Currently, a shelter has been placed over the sarcophagus to permit public viewing. The tumulus is approximately a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Kumasaki Station|Kumasaki Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki)]][[ja:下山古墳 (臼杵市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11360813]] Wikidata name: Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Shimoyama Kofun | native_name =下山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki).jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shimoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.13266667|N|131.7925|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Usuki, Ōita|Usuki, Ōita]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|07|57.6|N|131|47|33|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Shimoyama Kofun'''|下山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Shibao neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Usuki, Ōita|Usuki, Ōita]], on the island of [[Kyushu]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/203066|title= 下山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shimoyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a hill at an elevation of approximately 70 meters overlooking the Kumazaki River. Its total length is 68 meters, with a posterior circular portion 46-meters in diameter, and a rectangular anterior portion 37 meters wide. Traces of a partial moat encircle the posterior portion and there is a ''baizuka'' ancillary tumulus. Near the boundary between the anterior and posterior portions, is a heavily damaged stone statue depicting warrior in armor. There are only two ''kofun'' in Ōita Prefecture that have such a statue: Shimoyama Kofun, and Usuzuka Kofun, also located in Usuki. In the posterior circular portion is a house-shaped sarcophagus measuring 2.5 meters long, 1.3 meters wide, and 1 meter high, the lid of which has a band pattern embossed on it. When [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1951, the remains of two bodies, one male and one female, were found within the coffin, and the [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included a divine beast [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]], a shell ring, cylindrical beads, a comb, an iron sword and iron arrowheads, an iron ax, etc. A large amount of iron material, believed to be unprocessed iron, was found outside the coffin. From these grave goods it is estimated that it was built in the middle of the 5th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Currently, a shelter has been placed over the sarcophagus to permit public viewing. The tumulus is approximately a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Kumasaki Station|Kumasaki Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.usuki.oita.jp/docs/2014020500936/ Usuki City home page]{{in lang|ja}}

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/203066|title= 下山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shimoyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a hill at an elevation of approximately 70 meters overlooking the Kumazaki River. Its total length is 68 meters, with a posterior circular portion 46-meters in diameter, and a rectangular anterior portion 37 meters wide. Traces of a partial moat encircle the posterior portion and there is a ''baizuka'' ancillary tumulus. Near the boundary between the anterior and posterior portions, is a heavily damaged stone statue depicting warrior in armor. There are only two ''kofun'' in Ōita Prefecture that have such a statue: Shimoyama Kofun, and Usuzuka Kofun, also located in Usuki. In the posterior circular portion is a house-shaped sarcophagus measuring 2.5 meters long, 1.3 meters wide, and 1 meter high, the lid of which has a band pattern embossed on it. When [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1951, the remains of two bodies, one male and one female, were found within the coffin, and the [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included a divine beast [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]], a shell ring, cylindrical beads, a comb, an iron sword and iron arrowheads, an iron ax, etc. A large amount of iron material, believed to be unprocessed iron, was found outside the coffin. From these grave goods it is estimated that it was built in the middle of the 5th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Currently, a shelter has been placed over the sarcophagus to permit public viewing. The tumulus is approximately a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Kumasaki Station|Kumasaki Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki)]][[ja:下山古墳 (臼杵市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki)]][[ja:下山古墳 (臼杵市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki)]][[ja:下山古墳 (臼杵市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11360813]] Wikidata name: Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Shimoyama Kofun | native_name =下山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki).jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shimoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.13266667|N|131.7925|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Usuki, Ōita|Usuki, Ōita]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|07|57.6|N|131|47|33|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Shimoyama Kofun'''|下山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Shibao neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Usuki, Ōita|Usuki, Ōita]], on the island of [[Kyushu]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/203066|title= 下山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shimoyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a hill at an elevation of approximately 70 meters overlooking the Kumazaki River. Its total length is 68 meters, with a posterior circular portion 46-meters in diameter, and a rectangular anterior portion 37 meters wide. Traces of a partial moat encircle the posterior portion and there is a ''baizuka'' ancillary tumulus. Near the boundary between the anterior and posterior portions, is a heavily damaged stone statue depicting warrior in armor. There are only two ''kofun'' in Ōita Prefecture that have such a statue: Shimoyama Kofun, and Usuzuka Kofun, also located in Usuki. In the posterior circular portion is a house-shaped sarcophagus measuring 2.5 meters long, 1.3 meters wide, and 1 meter high, the lid of which has a band pattern embossed on it. When [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1951, the remains of two bodies, one male and one female, were found within the coffin, and the [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included a divine beast [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]], a shell ring, cylindrical beads, a comb, an iron sword and iron arrowheads, an iron ax, etc. A large amount of iron material, believed to be unprocessed iron, was found outside the coffin. From these grave goods it is estimated that it was built in the middle of the 5th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Currently, a shelter has been placed over the sarcophagus to permit public viewing. The tumulus is approximately a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Kumasaki Station|Kumasaki Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)]]

{{nihongo|'''Shimoyama Kofun'''|下山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Shibao neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Usuki, Ōita|Usuki, Ōita]], on the island of [[Kyushu]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/203066|title= 下山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shimoyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a hill at an elevation of approximately 70 meters overlooking the Kumazaki River. Its total length is 68 meters, with a posterior circular portion 46-meters in diameter, and a rectangular anterior portion 37 meters wide. Traces of a partial moat encircle the posterior portion and there is a ''baizuka'' ancillary tumulus. Near the boundary between the anterior and posterior portions, is a heavily damaged stone statue depicting warrior in armor. There are only two ''kofun'' in Ōita Prefecture that have such a statue: Shimoyama Kofun, and Usuzuka Kofun, also located in Usuki. In the posterior circular portion is a house-shaped sarcophagus measuring 2.5 meters long, 1.3 meters wide, and 1 meter high, the lid of which has a band pattern embossed on it. When [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1951, the remains of two bodies, one male and one female, were found within the coffin, and the [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included a divine beast [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]], a shell ring, cylindrical beads, a comb, an iron sword and iron arrowheads, an iron ax, etc. A large amount of iron material, believed to be unprocessed iron, was found outside the coffin. From these grave goods it is estimated that it was built in the middle of the 5th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Currently, a shelter has been placed over the sarcophagus to permit public viewing. The tumulus is approximately a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Kumasaki Station|Kumasaki Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.usuki.oita.jp/docs/2014020500936/ Usuki City home page]{{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki)]][[ja:下山古墳 (臼杵市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki)]][[ja:下山古墳 (臼杵市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki)]][[ja:下山古墳 (臼杵市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11360813]] Wikidata name: Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} | name =Shimoyama Kofun | native_name =下山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki).jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shimoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.13266667|N|131.7925|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Usuki, Ōita|en|Usuki, Ōita|WD=Q845792|lt=Usuki, Ōita}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|07|57.6|N|131|47|33|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Shimoyama Kofun'''|下山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped Kofun}}, located in the Shibao neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Usuki, Ōita|en|Usuki, Ōita|WD=Q845792|lt=Usuki, Ōita}}, on the island of [[Kyushu]] {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/203066|title= 下山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shimoyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a hill at an elevation of approximately 70 meters overlooking the Kumazaki River. Its total length is 68 meters, with a posterior circular portion 46-meters in diameter, and a rectangular anterior portion 37 meters wide. Traces of a partial moat encircle the posterior portion and there is a ''baizuka'' ancillary tumulus. Near the boundary between the anterior and posterior portions, is a heavily damaged stone statue depicting warrior in armor. There are only two ''kofun'' in Ōita Prefecture that have such a statue: Shimoyama Kofun, and Usuzuka Kofun, also located in Usuki. In the posterior circular portion is a house-shaped sarcophagus measuring 2.5 meters long, 1.3 meters wide, and 1 meter high, the lid of which has a band pattern embossed on it. When [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1951, the remains of two bodies, one male and one female, were found within the coffin, and the {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} included a divine beast {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}}, a shell ring, cylindrical beads, a comb, an iron sword and iron arrowheads, an iron ax, etc. A large amount of iron material, believed to be unprocessed iron, was found outside the coffin. From these grave goods it is estimated that it was built in the middle of the 5th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Currently, a shelter has been placed over the sarcophagus to permit public viewing. The tumulus is approximately a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kumasaki Station|en|Kumasaki Station|WD=Q5358533|lt=Kumasaki Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nippō Main Line|en|Nippō Main Line|WD=Q1052288|lt=Nippō Main Line}}. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|WD=Q6573294|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.usuki.oita.jp/docs/2014020500936/ Usuki City home page]{{in lang|ja}}

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | name =Shimoyama Kofun | native_name =下山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki).jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shimoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.13266667|N|131.7925|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Usuki, Ōita|en|Usuki, Ōita|WD=Q845792|lt=Usuki, Ōita|1=Usuki, Ōita}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|07|57.6|N|131|47|33|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Shimoyama Kofun'''|下山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped Kofun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, located in the Shibao neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Usuki, Ōita|en|Usuki, Ōita|WD=Q845792|lt=Usuki, Ōita|1=Usuki, Ōita}}, on the island of [[Kyushu]] {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/203066|title= 下山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shimoyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a hill at an elevation of approximately 70 meters overlooking the Kumazaki River. Its total length is 68 meters, with a posterior circular portion 46-meters in diameter, and a rectangular anterior portion 37 meters wide. Traces of a partial moat encircle the posterior portion and there is a ''baizuka'' ancillary tumulus. Near the boundary between the anterior and posterior portions, is a heavily damaged stone statue depicting warrior in armor. There are only two ''kofun'' in Ōita Prefecture that have such a statue: Shimoyama Kofun, and Usuzuka Kofun, also located in Usuki. In the posterior circular portion is a house-shaped sarcophagus measuring 2.5 meters long, 1.3 meters wide, and 1 meter high, the lid of which has a band pattern embossed on it. When [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1951, the remains of two bodies, one male and one female, were found within the coffin, and the {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} included a divine beast {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}, a shell ring, cylindrical beads, a comb, an iron sword and iron arrowheads, an iron ax, etc. A large amount of iron material, believed to be unprocessed iron, was found outside the coffin. From these grave goods it is estimated that it was built in the middle of the 5th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Currently, a shelter has been placed over the sarcophagus to permit public viewing. The tumulus is approximately a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kumasaki Station|en|Kumasaki Station|WD=Q5358533|lt=Kumasaki Station|1=Kumasaki Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nippō Main Line|en|Nippō Main Line|WD=Q1052288|lt=Nippō Main Line|1=Nippō Main Line}}. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|WD=Q6573294|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.usuki.oita.jp/docs/2014020500936/ Usuki City home page]{{in lang|ja}}

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpōkōenfun|en|Zenpōkōenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | name =Shimoyama Kofun | native_name =下山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shimoyama Kofun (Usuki).jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shimoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.13266667|N|131.7925|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Usuki, Ōita|en|Usuki, Ōita|WD=Q845792|lt=Usuki, Ōita|1=Usuki, Ōita}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|07|57.6|N|131|47|33|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Shimoyama Kofun'''|下山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpōkōenfun|en|Zenpōkōenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped Kofun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, located in the Shibao neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Usuki, Ōita|en|Usuki, Ōita|WD=Q845792|lt=Usuki, Ōita|1=Usuki, Ōita}}, on the island of [[Kyushu]] {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/203066|title= 下山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shimoyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpōkōenfun|1=[[Zenpōkōenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a hill at an elevation of approximately 70 meters overlooking the Kumazaki River. Its total length is 68 meters, with a posterior circular portion 46-meters in diameter, and a rectangular anterior portion 37 meters wide. Traces of a partial moat encircle the posterior portion and there is a ''baizuka'' ancillary tumulus. Near the boundary between the anterior and posterior portions, is a heavily damaged stone statue depicting warrior in armor. There are only two ''kofun'' in Ōita Prefecture that have such a statue: Shimoyama Kofun, and Usuzuka Kofun, also located in Usuki. In the posterior circular portion is a house-shaped sarcophagus measuring 2.5 meters long, 1.3 meters wide, and 1 meter high, the lid of which has a band pattern embossed on it. When [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1951, the remains of two bodies, one male and one female, were found within the coffin, and the {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} included a divine beast {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}, a shell ring, cylindrical beads, a comb, an iron sword and iron arrowheads, an iron ax, etc. A large amount of iron material, believed to be unprocessed iron, was found outside the coffin. From these grave goods it is estimated that it was built in the middle of the 5th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Currently, a shelter has been placed over the sarcophagus to permit public viewing. The tumulus is approximately a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kumasaki Station|en|Kumasaki Station|WD=Q5358533|lt=Kumasaki Station|1=Kumasaki Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nippō Main Line|en|Nippō Main Line|WD=Q1052288|lt=Nippō Main Line|1=Nippō Main Line}}. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|WD=Q6573294|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.usuki.oita.jp/docs/2014020500936/ Usuki City home page]{{in lang|ja}}
